things.name: Tabriz Historic Bazaar Complex
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage world's largest covered bazaar, trading since the 13th century

things.name: Blue Mosque (Masjid-e Kabud)
things.description: 15th-century Timurid mosque with stunning blue faience tilework, known as the Turquoise of Islam

things.name: El-Goli (Shah Goli) Garden
things.description: 19th-century Qajar pleasure garden with an island pavilion on a large artificial lake

things.name: Azerbaijan Museum
things.description: Iran's finest regional museum with Urartian, Medes and Achaemenid artefacts

things.name: Tabriz Citadel (Arg of Tabriz)
things.description: Ruined 14th-century fortress whose remaining brick tower dominates the city centre

things.name: Kabud (Blue) Bridge
things.description: Historic Safavid bridge over the Mehranrood River

things.name: Iranian-Azerbaijani Constitution House
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage house where the Iranian Constitutional Revolution was declared in 1906

things.name: Kandovan Rock Village
things.description: People still living in 700-year-old volcanic rock cone dwellings 50km south of Tabriz

things.name: Mount Sahand Day Trip
things.description: Ski resort and summer hiking on the 3,710m volcano south of Tabriz

things.name: Tabriz Carpet Workshops
things.description: World-famous double-weft Persian carpets woven in workshops throughout the city
